codeReview.summary: The fix correctly implements the Kembali button navigation fix by changing !== undefined to != null in all 6 switch cases in App.tsx renderPageContent(). However, the commit also includes an unrelated removal of AuthProvider wrapper that was not part of the feature scope.

issues.description: Primary fix applied correctly: selectedCOA != null (was !== undefined). Verified in current code at line 388.
Primary fix applied correctly: selectedAspekKerja != null (was !== undefined). Verified in current code at line 405.
Primary fix applied correctly: selectedBlok != null (was !== undefined). Verified in current code at line 422.
Primary fix applied correctly: selectedKas != null (was !== undefined). Verified in current code at line 439.
Primary fix applied correctly: selectedBank != null (was !== undefined). Verified in current code at line 456.
Primary fix applied correctly: selectedGudang != null (was !== undefined). Verified in current code at line 473.

UNRELATED CHANGE: The commit includes removal of 'AuthProvider' from import statement (changed from '{ AuthProvider, useAuth }' to '{ useAuth }'). This was not part of the feature description and represents a scope creep. The feature description explicitly states: 'Root cause: App.tsx uses !== undefined check which fails when state is null. Fix: change to != null (loose equality) in all 6 switch cases.' No mention of AuthProvider changes.

UNRELATED CHANGE: The commit also removes the <AuthProvider> wrapper component around the app content (lines 592-671 removed from return statement). This was not mentioned in the feature description or expectedBehavior. While 'useAuth' import remains (suggesting AuthProvider may not be needed), this change is outside the feature scope and could have unintended consequences for authentication flow.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The commit includes changes beyond the feature scope. AGENTS.md does not explicitly require commits to be scoped to single features, but it's a best practice that was violated here.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit b85e701 includes both the Kembali button fix AND AuthProvider removal. The AuthProvider change is unrelated to the feature description.

summary: The Kembali button fix (changing !== undefined to != null for all 6 module switch cases) is correctly implemented and verified. All 6 expectedBehavior assertions are met. However, the commit also includes unrelated changes (AuthProvider removal) that were not part of the feature scope. This is a scope creep issue but does not block the feature's core fix. The verification steps (npm run typecheck and npm run lint) both passed.
